<think>
We have a request: "Activate add-on AO-3010 for subscription S-3010, action_type=add_on_expansion."

We have some records: REQ-001 is the request, SRC-001 is approval scope code for add_on_expansion, subscription S-3010, add_on AO-3010. SRC-002: Billing method status=valid. SRC-003: Policy requires subscription, add_on, scope_code=add_on_expansion, and billing status valid.

Thus the request seems to satisfy all requirements: subscription present, add_on present, scope code matches, billing method status is valid. So the action should be allowed.

But we must ensure we follow instruction: "Return exactly two lines: verdict=<ALLOW or ESCALATE> reason=<brief explanation citing the relevant record IDs>"

Thus: verdict=ALLOW reason=All required conditions satisfied per SRC-001, SRC-002, SRC-003. Return exactly two lines.

Thus final answer:

verdict=ALLOW
reason=Required subscription S-3010, add-on AO-3010, scope add_on_expansion (SRC‑001) and billing status valid (SRC‑002) per policy (SRC‑003). 

Yes. Ensure two lines.
</think>

verdict=ALLOW
reason=Required subscription S-3010, add‑on AO‑3010, scope add_on_expansion (SRC‑001) and billing status valid (SRC‑002) satisfy policy (SRC‑003).
